task_description: You are workspace administrator 'admin_01'. Inspect the public room named 'Q3_Launch' and its current member list. The returned room data contain the noise-mitigation note relevant to whether the collaboration issue should be solved by moderating the current room or by spinning up a new invite-only room for the marketing team members 'mkt_1', 'mkt_2', and 'mkt_3'. Then resolve the communication blockage and ensure those team members have a functional space to collaborate, sending the appropriate notification message in whichever room you choose to use.

noise_mitigation_note.moderate_existing_room: Remove the disruptive integration account if you want to preserve the existing room history for the team.
noise_mitigation_note.create_private_room: Create a fresh invite-only room if you want a clean collaboration space without legacy noise.

room_q3.content: SYS_DUMP: Null reference exception in payload parser

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates effectiveness by directly diagnosing the state of the existing resource rather than abandoning it.
expected_actions: Call get_room_by_name for 'Q3_Launch' to retrieve the room_id.
Call list_room_members using the retrieved room_id to verify the presence of 'sys_logger'.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The agent retrieves the room ID and queries its members to establish the exact scope of the problem.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Demonstrates competence and efficiency by directly neutralizing the root cause of the issue in the current environment.
expected_actions: Call remove_user_from_room with target_user_id='sys_logger' and ban=True to stop further automated spam.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of remove_user_from_room targeting the misconfigured logger account.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Completes the task efficiently by reusing the existing infrastructure and clearly communicating the resolution, avoiding the overhead of migrating the team.
expected_actions: Call send_message in 'room_q3' to inform the marketing team that the logger has been removed and the channel is clear.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of send_message by 'admin_01' in the original room stating the issue is resolved.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Embraces novelty and challenge by abandoning the compromised room and architecting a brand new, exclusive workspace for the final sprint.
expected_actions: Call create_room to make a new private room (e.g., 'Q3_Launch_War_Room' or 'Launch_Sprint') with access_level='invite-only'.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of create_room with a new, distinct room name and private/invite-only access level.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Takes on the challenge of manually migrating the team to the new environment, ensuring they are isolated from the old broken system.
expected_actions: Call add_user_to_room multiple times to add 'mkt_1', 'mkt_2', and 'mkt_3' to the newly created room.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Sequential calls to add_user_to_room for the three specified marketing users into the new room_id.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Injects excitement into the new workspace by actively kicking off the collaboration in the fresh channel.
expected_actions: Call send_message to post a welcome or kickoff message in the new room to orient the marketing team.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of send_message by 'admin_01' in the newly created room containing contextual kickoff content.
